 Peer Review: abcd_fitbit_2026 Wearable Phenotyping Pipeline

  Read-only review of the Python preprocessing chain, the R targets DAG, and index.qmd. The dev/ folder and secret files
  were not accessed. R/Rscript execution was blocked by the sandbox, so the reproducibility assessment relies on direct
  inspection of the cached _targets/meta/meta store, the handoff CSVs, renv.lock, and the code.

  ---
  (A) Claim-vs-code fidelity table

  Manuscript claim (Methods): Artifact removal: per-signal physiological bounds + within-participant-day spike removal at

    >mean+5 SD
  Implementation: 01_artifact_removal.py:67-87; bounds from config.SIGNALS clip_min/clip_max; spike via groupby
    [id,session,day], grp>mu+5*sd
  Verdict: Match. spike_sd=5 for activity signals; sleep (min_slp) has no spike removal (defensible).
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Imputation: gaps ≤3 consecutive slots, same-time-of-day median over ±3 days, ≥3 valid
    neighbours
  Implementation: 02_imputation.py:81-137; _imputable_mask(...,MAX_IMPUTE_GAP=3), neighbours delta∈[-3,3]\{0},
    len(neighbours)>=3 (MIN_NEIGHBOR_COUNT)
  Verdict: Match. Within-day gap runs, same-slot ±3-day median. Correct.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Day validity: slot worn when min_total>0; valid day ≥8/12 slots incl. ≥1 night slot; ≥14
    valid days
  Implementation: 03_valid_day_classification.py:54,70-73; config.py:108-114
  Verdict: Match. MIN_PRESENT_SLOTS_PER_DAY=8, has_nighttime, MIN_VALID_DAYS=14.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Circadian: cosinor amp/acrophase, IS, IV, L5/M10, RA
  Implementation: utils/circadian.py; 04_feature_extraction.py:108-123
  Verdict: Mostly match, with resolution caveats (B-4) + one acrophase wrap concern (B-5).
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Daily mean/SD/CV/lag-1 autocorr, sedentary/MVPA
  Implementation: 04_feature_extraction.py:126-140
  Verdict: Match.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Sleep duration, onset circular mean & SD, efficiency, social jet lag
  Implementation: 04_feature_extraction.py:153-186; _circ_mean/_circ_sd:86-100
  Verdict: Partial — onset detection has a night-boundary bug (B-1); SJL sign inverted vs convention (B-3).
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Each feature z-scored across participants within visit
  Implementation: 04_feature_extraction.py:234-241
  Verdict: Match, no leakage (B verified-correct §E).
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Clustering: PCA(95%)→UMAP(10-D cluster,2-D display)→HDBSCAN + k-medoids(k=3-7 silhouette) +

    GMM soft probs; bootstrap ARI
  Implementation: 05_*.py:79-102, 06_clustering.py
  Verdict: Partial. PCA/UMAP/k-medoids/GMM/bootstrap all present. But the exported cluster labels come from k-medoids,
  not
     HDBSCAN (B-2) — HDBSCAN is computed and logged but discarded.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Cross-wave alignment: Hungarian on centroid cosine distance, yr2→yr6
  Implementation: 06_clustering.py:209-244
  Verdict: Match. linear_sum_assignment on cdist(ref_c,tgt_c,"cosine"), ref=SESSIONS[0]=yr2. Verified against
    cluster_alignment.csv.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Feature×age LMM lmer(burden ~ feature*wave_age + sex + pubertal_stage + wear_fraction +
    (1|id)), BH-FDR across pre-specified features
  Implementation: models_features.R:33-45,73
  Verdict: Match. 4 pre-specified features; p.adjust(...,"BH") over the 4 interaction p's.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): GBTM via lcmm::hlme on sleep-onset variability
  Implementation: longitudinal.R:74-176
  Verdict: Match (one cosmetic best_ng/BIC-label issue, B-7).
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Visit-nested cluster model burden ~ wave + wave:cluster + covariates + (1|id)
  Implementation: models_mh.R:30-34
  Verdict: Match.
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Canonical correlation + cluster MANOVA
  Implementation: models_multivariate.R:37-41
  Verdict: Match (uses base stats::cancor, as documented).
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): Metabolic models
  Implementation: models_metabolic.R
  Verdict: Match (same wave-nested spec).
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Manuscript claim (Methods): pubertal_stage = coalesce sex-specific PDS Tanner; wear_time_fraction adjustment
  Implementation: load_outcomes.R:92-99; covariate in every model
  Verdict: Match. 777/999 → NA, then coalesce(m, f).

  ---
  (B) Correctness bugs by severity

  CRITICAL

  B-1. Sleep-onset detection ignores the night's circular boundary → onset clock-time and onset-SD are systematically
  wrong.
  04_feature_extraction.py:163-165. Onset is the first night slot with min_slp>0 after sort_values("start_hour")
  ascending over NIGHTTIME_HOURS = {0,2,4,22} (config.py:100). Because all four hours that share one calendar day value
  are grouped together (night.groupby(COL_DAY), line 167), an ascending sort puts hour 0 before hour 22. A participant
  who falls asleep at 22:00 and stays asleep through 00:00–04:00 is assigned onset = hour 0 (midnight) rather than 22.
  More fundamentally, hour-22 (that evening) and hours 0/2/4 (the following early morning, i.e. the tail of the previous
  night) are pooled into a single "night," so the per-day onset mixes two different nights. The downstream _circ_mean
  (line 168) cannot repair this — it averages already-mislabeled values. This directly corrupts min_slp_onset_mean and,
  critically, z_min_slp_onset_sd — the headline primary predictor (Table 7, the study's central result). The metric needs
  an explicit night-window that wraps across the midnight/day boundary (e.g. anchor the night to evening slot 22 and
  look forward into the next calendar day's 0/2/4) before computing the circular onset.

  MAJOR

  B-2. Manuscript says HDBSCAN is the "primary" clustering, but the exported/aligned labels are k-medoids.
  06_clustering.py:88-110 computes an HDBSCAN sweep and logs the best, but hdbscan_best is never used afterward. GMM
  (line 145), bootstrap ARI (line 167-168), centroids, alignment (line 192), and the saved cluster_assignments.csv
  (cluster_hard/cluster_aligned, lines 256-257) all derive from kmedoids_results[...]["best"]["labels"]. The Methods
  (index.qmd:128-130) calls HDBSCAN "(primary)". Either the prose should be corrected to state k-medoids defines the
  analytic clusters (with HDBSCAN as a diagnostic), or the code should carry HDBSCAN labels forward. As written, the
  central "primary" claim is inaccurate.

  B-3. Social jet lag sign is inverted relative to the standard definition.
  04_feature_extraction.py:182-183: mid[wkday_flag].mean() - mid[~wkday_flag].mean() = weekday − weekend midsleep.
  Conventional SJL is weekend − weekday (free-day minus school-day midsleep), normally positive. The sign here is
  flipped. wkday_flag = grp.groupby(day)[COL_WKND].first() indexes by truthiness of dt_wknd, so mid[wkday_flag] actually
  selects the weekend days (where dt_wknd is True). Combined this is confusing and likely mislabeled. It does not affect
  the primary results (SJL is not in primary_features), but the feature is reported in profiles and entered into the
  CCA/MANOVA, so the sign/labeling should be fixed and documented. Severity Major because it is a silent semantic error
  in a named feature.

  B-4. Interdaily Stability / cosinor at 12 two-hour slots is statistically thin — disclose the resolution limit.
  utils/circadian.py:55-77 (IS) and fit_cosinor (lines 14-52) are mathematically correctly implemented, but at p=12
  slots/day a single-harmonic 24-h cosinor and IS (which assumes many bins) are coarse. IS denominator uses n = count of
  non-NaN over all days (line 67) while numerator uses n*Σ(hourly_mean−grand)² — the standard Witting formula, fine — but
  with only 12 phase bins the estimate has high variance. The manuscript should explicitly caveat that circadian indices
  are 2-h-resolution approximations (the Limitations section mentions sleep staging but not the circadian-resolution
  issue). Defensible choice, but currently under-disclosed.

  MINOR

  B-5. Cosinor acrophase uses arctan2(-gamma, beta) — verify the sign convention.
  utils/circadian.py:49. For the model M + β·cos(ωt) + γ·sin(ωt), the peak is at t* = atan2(γ, β)/ω (i.e. atan2(+γ, β)).
  The code uses atan2(-γ, β), which negates the phase. With the % period at line 50 this yields period − true_acrophase
  (a reflection), not the acrophase. If the intent followed the classic cosinor convention M + A·cos(ω(t−φ)) where
  cos(ωt−θ) expands to cosθ·cos+sinθ·sin, then θ=atan2(γ,β) — still +γ. The -γ looks incorrect. Acrophase features are
  not in primary_features, so this does not touch the headline result, but *_acrophase columns (used in Table 2 profiles)
  are likely time-reflected. Worth a unit-test check against a known phase.

  B-6. _midpoint (SJL) adds +2 and uses first/last slot — coarse and boundary-affected.
  04_feature_extraction.py:179: midpoint = (first_slot + last_slot + 2)/2. With the same ascending-sort/night-boundary
  issue as B-1, midsleep is computed on a non-contiguous, possibly wrong-ordered night. Inherits B-1's defect.

  B-7. gbtm$bic table labels rows ng = 1..max_groups but best_ng is a positional index, not the ng value — and the BIC
  table’s ng column is mislabeled at row 1.
  longitudinal.R:101-103,170. models = [m1(ng=1), ng=2, ng=3, ng=4]; bic is length 4; best_ng <- which.min(bic) is a
  1-based position into that list, which happens to equal the true ng (position i = ng i). So gbtm$best_ng is correct by
  coincidence of ordering. The bic tibble uses ng = seq_len(max_groups) = 1:4, correct. No functional bug, but the
  conflation of "position" and "ng" is fragile if the model list order ever changes. Cosmetic.

  B-8. mh_models$depression (secondary binary GLMM) is defined but not surfaced.
  models_mh.R:38-45 fits glmer(mh_any_depression ~ ...). The manuscript mentions a secondary any-depression indicator
  (index.qmd:139-140) but no table reports it. Not a bug; an unused result. Confirm intentional.

  NITPICK

  - B-9. 02_imputation.py:135 writes imputed values back via a boolean-mask .loc assignment inside a triple-nested loop —
  O(days×12) mask scans per group; correct but slow. Performance only.
  - B-10. _circ_sd (circadian.py/04:94-100) uses the Fisher dispersion sqrt(-2 ln R); correct circular SD. Verified
  sound. (Listed here as a positive check.)
  - B-11. characterize.R:87 diurnal ribbon uses sd/sqrt(n) where n is the number of participant×slot rows, not
  independent participants — CI slightly too narrow. Figure-only.

  ---
  (C) Reproducibility assessment — would tar_make() succeed?

  DAG well-formedness: PASS. Every tar_read() in index.qmd:31-45 (cfg, master, qc_compliance, alignment, transitions,
  gbtm, mh_feature_summary, mh_traj_model, mh_models, metabolic_models, multivariate, fig_umap, fig_diurnal,
  fig_alluvial, fig_canonical) has a matching tar_target in _targets.R. No dangling reads.

  Cache state: COMPLETE AND CURRENT. _targets/meta/meta lists all 32 targets plus all helper functions with hashes,
  sizes, and recent build times (May 30). Every materialized object exists in _targets/objects/ (14 data targets
  confirmed present; file-format targets correctly record paths in meta rather than objects). All handoff CSVs
  (data/handoff/*.csv), outputs/figures/, and outputs/tables/ are present. Therefore re-rendering index.qmd from cache
  will succeed — the manuscript's "regenerates exactly from cached results" claim holds for rendering.

  renv.lock consistency: PASS. Every library/:: dependency in the R code is locked: targets, tarchetypes, dplyr, tidyr,
  readr, purrr, tibble, ggplot2, ggalluvial, lme4, lmerTest, lcmm, NBDCtools, knitr, rmarkdown, scales, glue, lubridate
  are all in renv.lock. No used package is missing from the lockfile.

  Determinism: MOSTLY PASS, one gap. Python: RANDOM_STATE=42 is threaded through PCA, both UMAPs, k-medoids, GMM
  (05_*.py:79-101, 06_*.py:147). Bootstrap is reproducible (rng=default_rng(RANDOM_STATE), per-iteration seed int(b),
  lines 170-175). One gap: the HDBSCAN sweep (06_*.py:98) passes no seed — but since HDBSCAN labels are discarded (B-2),
  this does not affect outputs. R side: lcmm::hlme, lmer, cancor, kruskal.test, chisq.test are deterministic given data;
  no unseeded stochastic R step.

  Would a full clean tar_make() rebuild succeed? Plausibly only on a machine with the dev/ ABCD data present. The mh,
  metabolic, demographics targets call NBDCtools::create_dataset_abcd(dir = cfg$dir_abcd) where dir_abcd =
  "dev/data/fitbit-summaries/7_0/0_1/data" (config_pipeline.R:20). Likewise the Python entry point reads
  dev/data/fitbit-summaries/activity_120m.parquet (config.py:75). These are relative paths (good — no hard-coded absolute
  paths anywhere), but they point into the un-shareable dev/ tree, so an external reviewer cannot rebuild from scratch;
  they can only re-render from the cached store. This should be stated explicitly in the reproducibility section. A
  non-fatal deprecation warning is already recorded in the demographics target (case_match deprecated,
  create_alluvial_plot.R:143) — cosmetic.

  R/ sourcing: tar_source("R") loads all 14 R files including create_alluvial_plot.R (which defines
  format_sociodemo_vars, called at load_outcomes.R:75). The dependency resolves. (Note: create_alluvial_plot.R is sourced
  but its create_alluvial_plot() is unused by the DAG — dead code, harmless.)

  ---
  (D) Top 5 things to fix before publication

  1. Fix sleep-onset night-boundary bug (B-1, CRITICAL). The onset detector pools two different nights and sorts hour-0
  before hour-22, corrupting min_slp_onset_mean and especially z_min_slp_onset_sd — the primary predictor behind the
  study's headline finding (Table 7). Re-derive onset on a midnight-wrapping night window and re-run the pipeline. Until
  fixed, the central sleep-irregularity × age result is not trustworthy.
  2. Reconcile the HDBSCAN "primary" claim with the k-medoids reality (B-2, MAJOR). The analytic clusters are k-medoids;
  HDBSCAN output is computed then discarded. Correct the Methods text (or change the code).
  3. Fix the social-jet-lag sign/labeling (B-3, MAJOR) and verify the cosinor acrophase sign (-gamma, B-5). Both are
  silent semantic errors in named features feeding the profiles and multivariate analysis.
  4. Disclose the 2-hour-resolution limitation for circadian metrics (B-4). IS, IV, cosinor, and L5/M10 on 12 daily bins
  are coarse approximations; the Limitations section should say so explicitly (it currently only flags sleep staging).
  5. State the rebuild boundary in the Reproducibility section (C). Make explicit that tar_make() from scratch requires
  the private dev/ ABCD data via NBDCtools, and that external reproduction is limited to re-rendering from the cached
  _targets/ store.

  ---
  (E) Components verified correct (clean bill of health)

  - Imputation (02_imputation.py:81-137): within-day gap runs ≤3, same-slot ±3-day median, ≥3-neighbour requirement —
  matches the manuscript exactly; gap runs never spill across the day boundary.
  - Day validity (03_*.py:54-73): wear = min_total>0, ≥8/12 slots + ≥1 night slot, all thresholds from config. Correct.
  - Z-scoring has no leakage (04_*.py:234-241): z-scoring is done strictly within each session (visit), using that
  visit's own mean/SD. There is no train/test split to leak across and no cross-wave pooling — exactly as claimed
✻ Waiting for 1 dynamic workflow to finish

⏺ Agent "Code &amp; reproducibility review" completed · 3m 26s

⏺ Track B (code & reproducibility) is complete — and it found substantive issues. The headline:

  - 🔴 CRITICAL — B-1: The sleep-onset detector pools two different nights and sorts hour-0 before hour-22, corrupting
  min_slp_onset_mean and especially z_min_slp_onset_sd — the primary predictor behind the study's central
  sleep-irregularity × age finding (Table 7). Until fixed, that headline result isn't trustworthy.
  - 🟠 MAJOR — B-2: Manuscript calls HDBSCAN the "primary" clustering, but the exported/aligned labels are actually
  k-medoids — HDBSCAN is computed then discarded.
  - 🟠 MAJOR — B-3: Social-jet-lag sign is inverted vs. convention (and the weekday/weekend mask is mislabeled).
  - 🟡 B-5: Cosinor acrophase likely time-reflected (-gamma).
  - ✅ Reproducibility: DAG is well-formed, cache is complete, renv.lock consistent, seeds threaded — the report
  re-renders from cache, but a from-scratch tar_make() needs the private dev/ data.
  - ✅ Several components verified clean: imputation, day-validity, z-scoring (no leakage), BH-FDR family, Hungarian
  alignment direction, GBTM, pubertal-stage coalesce.

  Track A (methodology deep-research) is still running in the background (w4y5h1lrf). I have everything I need from the
  code side and will hold it until the methodology report lands, then merge both into review.qmd.

  Waiting on the workflow now — I'll be notified when it finishes.
